metadata.title: Harmonized Structure (HS) clause mapping for the seven ISO management system standards in scope of the Telin GRC Suite mock-up
metadata.harmonized_structure_canonical_reference: ISO/IEC Directives Part 1, Consolidated ISO Supplement, Annex SL, Appendix 2 (renamed Harmonized Structure in May 2021; updated 2022; climate-change amendment Feb 2024)
metadata.disclaimer: This file is a structural skeleton reconstructed from secondary authoritative summaries (BSI, DNV, DQS, NQA, SGS, ANSI, Advisera, PECB, Amtivo). It is NOT a substitute for the normative text of the ISO standards, which are copyrighted and must be obtained from ISO Store / ANSI / BSI Knowledge. A formal certification body (e.g. Intertek, BSI, DNV, TUEV, SGS, BV) will validate every clause-to-clause mapping against the purchased normative text during their gap analysis and stage-1/stage-2 audits. Use this file for ideation and brainstorming only.
metadata.scope_disclaimer: Mock-up — Brainstorming simulation. No data from Telin is used. ISO Crosswalk content may vary across iterations.

subclauses.title: Understanding the organization and its context
subclauses.notes: Feb 2024 amendment: organization shall determine whether climate change is a relevant issue.

subclauses.title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
subclauses.notes: 2022 HS expanded 4.2(c): identify which interested-party requirements will be addressed via the MS. 2024 amendment note: interested parties may have climate-related requirements.

subclauses.title: Determining the scope of the XXX management system
Actions to address risks and opportunities
XXX objectives and planning to achieve them
Planning of changes
subclauses.notes: NEW in 2021/2022 HS (was not in 2012 HLS).

subclauses.title: Operational planning and control
subclauses.notes: 2022 HS replaced 'outsourced processes' with 'externally provided processes, products, services'.

subclauses.title: Continual improvement
subclauses.notes: 2022 HS swapped order: continual improvement now precedes nonconformity (was 10.2 in 2012 HLS).

subclauses.title: Nonconformity and corrective action
subclauses.notes: 2022 HS swapped order: was 10.1 in 2012 HLS.

standards.name: Information Security Management Systems (ISMS) — Requirements
standards.hs_revision_followed: 2022 HS (early adopter); Feb 2024 climate amendment incorporated via Amd 1:2024

annex_controls.title: Information security controls reference (normative)
annex_controls.delta_from_2013_edition: Restructured from 114 controls / 14 domains; 11 net-new controls; 24 merged; 58 retained

standards.deviation_notes: 10.1/10.2 swapped per 2022 HS. Clause 4.4 explicitly requires process orientation.

standards.name: Privacy Information Management System (PIMS) — Extension to ISO/IEC 27001 and 27002
standards.hs_revision_followed: Inherits whichever HS revision its base ISO/IEC 27001 edition uses (so as of 2026, the 2022 HS via 27001:2022).
standards.structure_note: 27701 is NOT a standalone management system. It augments 27001 with privacy-specific requirements and adds privacy-specific controls layered onto 27002.

extensions_beyond_hs.title: PIMS-specific requirements relating to ISO/IEC 27001 (clause-by-clause augmentation of 27001 clauses 4-10)
PIMS-specific guidance relating to ISO/IEC 27002 controls
Additional ISO/IEC 27002 guidance for PII Controllers
Additional ISO/IEC 27002 guidance for PII Processors

annex_controls.annex_F_note: Annex F provides the mapping between PIMS controls and ISO/IEC 27001/27002 controls.

standards.deviation_notes: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 revision is in progress (rebasing on 27001:2022 with restructured annexes). This entry reflects the 2019 edition; verify currency before audit.

standards.name: Business Continuity Management Systems (BCMS) — Requirements

extensions_beyond_hs.title: Business impact analysis and risk assessment
Business continuity strategies and solutions
extensions_beyond_hs.notes: Renamed from 'Business continuity strategy' in 2012 edition.

extensions_beyond_hs.title: Evaluation of business continuity documentation and capabilities

standards.deviation_notes: Old 10.1/10.2 order: 10.1 Nonconformity and corrective action, 10.2 Continual improvement. No 6.3 'Planning of changes' subclause. Climate amendment may apply if BSI/Intertek issue corrigenda; verify.

standards.name: Anti-bribery Management Systems (ABMS) — Requirements

extensions_beyond_hs.title: Implementation of anti-bribery controls by controlled organizations and by business associates
Gifts, hospitality, donations and similar benefits

standards.deviation_notes: Old 10.1/10.2 order. Superseded by ISO 37001:2025 (transition deadline 2027-02-28).

standards.name: Anti-bribery Management Systems (ABMS) — Requirements (revised)

extensions_beyond_hs.title: Bribery-specific operational controls (8.2 due diligence, 8.3 financial controls, 8.4 non-financial controls, 8.5 controls by controlled orgs / business associates, 8.6 commitments, 8.7 gifts/hospitality, 8.8 detection of inadequacy, 8.9 raising concerns, 8.10 investigation)

standards.deviation_notes: 10.1/10.2 swapped per 2022 HS. Terminology: 'interested parties' replaces 'stakeholders' in line with HS. Expanded conflict-of-interest requirements.

standards.name: Occupational Health and Safety Management Systems (OH&S MS) — Requirements with guidance for use

extensions_beyond_hs.title: Consultation and participation of workers
extensions_beyond_hs.notes: Clause 5 retitled 'Leadership and worker participation' (vs HS 'Leadership' alone).

extensions_beyond_hs.title: Hazard identification and assessment of risks and opportunities
Determination of legal requirements and other requirements
Eliminating hazards and reducing OH&S risks

standards.deviation_notes: Clause 10.2 covers 'Incident, nonconformity and corrective action' — incidents added relative to HS. Old 10.1/10.2 order.

standards.name: Energy Management Systems (EnMS) — Requirements with guidance for use

extensions_beyond_hs.title: Energy review
extensions_beyond_hs.notes: WARNING: 50001's 6.3 'Energy review' is UNRELATED to the 2022 HS's 6.3 'Planning of changes'. Number collision; semantic divergence.

extensions_beyond_hs.title: Planning for collection of energy data
Design (energy-efficient design of facilities, equipment, systems, processes)
Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation of energy performance and the EnMS

standards.deviation_notes: Old 10.1/10.2 order. Numerous clause-6 EnMS extensions create high deviation from baseline HS skeleton.

standards.name: AI Management System (AIMS) — Requirements

extensions_beyond_hs.title: AI system impact assessment
extensions_beyond_hs.notes: Unique to 42001 — assesses impact on individuals, groups, society.

annex_C.title: Potential AI-related organizational objectives and risk sources

annex_D.title: Use of AI management system across domains and sectors

standards.deviation_notes: 10.1/10.2 swapped per 2022 HS. Designed to be co-implementable with ISO/IEC 27001 (information security) and ISO/IEC 27701 (privacy).

methodology.intro: How a certification body (Intertek, BSI, DNV, TUEV, SGS, BV) constructs and validates a multi-standard crosswalk in practice. This file follows the same four-step approach.

steps.name: Anchor on the Harmonized Structure as common spine
steps.detail: Load the canonical 10-clause / ~30-subclause HS skeleton (ISO/IEC Directives Part 1 Annex SL Appendix 2) as the master key set. Every target standard maps onto these keys first. This guarantees that clause 7.5 'Documented information' has a single shared definition across all 7 standards in scope.

steps.name: Layer standard-specific extensions
steps.detail: Append discipline-specific subclauses as child nodes under their parent HS clause. Examples in this file: ISO 22301 §8.2 BIA, ISO 37001 §4.5 bribery risk assessment, ISO 50001 §6.3 energy review (NOTE: collides numerically but not semantically with HS 2022 §6.3), ISO 45001 §5.4 worker participation, ISO 42001 §6.1.4 AI system impact assessment.

steps.name: Flag wording divergence
steps.detail: Even where clause numbers match, certification bodies tag clauses where the discipline standard adds qualifiers so auditors do not assume identical intent. Examples: ISO 27001 §4.4 explicitly demands 'process orientation'; ISO 45001 §10.2 covers 'Incident, nonconformity and corrective action' — adds incidents. These are surfaced in 'deviation_notes' per standard in this JSON.

steps.name: Overlay sector control catalogues
steps.detail: For standards with normative annexes (27001 Annex A, 27701 Annex F, 42001 Annex A-D, 37001 control measures embedded in clause 8) build a separate orthogonal map from main-body clauses to control IDs. This enables cross-framework controls reuse (one access-control control mapped to ISO 27001 A.5.15, ISO 27701 7.2.x, ISO 42001 A.x.x).

methodology.validation_path: Mock-up structural mapping (this file) -> Pre-audit gap analysis (using purchased normative texts) -> Stage-1 documentation review by accredited certification body (Intertek / BSI / DNV / TUEV / SGS / BV) -> Stage-2 implementation audit -> Certification decision -> Surveillance audits annually.

sources.use: intended HS explainer; no body content extractable

claims: list of standards on each version
Annex A: 93 controls / 4 themes vs 114 / 14 in 2013 edition

caveats: ISO standards are copyrighted. Full clause text and exact subclause titles MUST be obtained from purchased standards (ISO Store, ANSI, BSI Knowledge) before publishing or auditing against this file.

The canonical HS skeleton in this JSON is reconstructed from secondary sources because direct fetches of ISO Directives, BSI explainer pages, and Intertek's HS page either returned 403, 404, or navigation-only content. Verify subclause titles against ISO/IEC Directives Part 1 Annex SL Appendix 2 (current consolidated edition).

The Feb 2024 climate-change amendment applies retroactively to all published HS-based standards. Per-standard amendment status (corrigenda issued by ISO/SC committees) must be confirmed with the accreditation body before mapping is finalised.

ISO/IEC 27701 has a 2025 revision in progress (rebasing on ISO/IEC 27001:2022 with restructured annexes). Confirm current edition before audit. The 2019 entry in this file is a placeholder pending the 2025 release.
Do not claim a clause-text quote unless sourced from the paid standard. Mapping at the title-and-structure level (this file) is appropriate for ideation and gap planning; verbatim quoting is not.

Number collisions exist across standards (e.g. ISO 50001 §6.3 'Energy review' vs HS 2022 §6.3 'Planning of changes'). Number-only joins between standards will produce false positives; always join on (standard_id, clause_id, semantic_tag).
validation_authority: Final validation of any clause-by-clause crosswalk derived from this file is the responsibility of an accredited certification body. Intertek, BSI, DNV, TUEV, SGS, and BV are commonly engaged for this purpose. This file does not constitute audit evidence.
